assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains 'No channel' as legacy and details the missing granular maintenance exclusion types in the comparison table and throughout the explanation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No channel' only has 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusions and that per-nodepool maintenance exclusion granularity is limited, while release channels have full control

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response clearly states in the comparison table and text that Extended support (24 months) is available on release channels but not on 'No channel'

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response mentions rollout sequencing is not available on 'No channel' in the comparison table and states 'it doesn't support rollout sequencing' explicitly

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular channel as the target and explains why Regular matches closest to current 'No channel' upgrade pace, though it doesn't explicitly mention Stable channel behavior matching

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response has a dedicated section on Extended channel as an alternative, specifically mentioning it provides maximum control around EoS enforcement

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Response includes specific warning about exclusion translation, stating only 'no_upgrades' exclusions translate automatically, and provides a code example for temporary protection during migration

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple gcloud commands including 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel regular'
Response does not reference or link to the official GKE documentation comparison table at cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels
